title: Bee Removal in Flatbush, Brooklyn | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Flatbush, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bee extraction from homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for $350-$550 service!
intro: Best Pest Control NYC provides expert bee removal services throughout Flatbush, Brooklyn, protecting both residential and commercial properties from unwanted hives. Our licensed technicians safely remove bee colonies from Victorian homes, brick townhouses, and multi-family buildings that define this historic neighborhood. We offer humane, effective bee removal with pricing between $350 and $550, depending on hive location and complexity.
whyUs: With over a decade serving Flatbush residents, we understand the unique challenges of bee infestations in Brooklyn's dense urban environment. Our team responds quickly, uses eco-friendly methods, and ensures complete hive removal to prevent future swarms from returning to your property.
localContext: Flatbush's tree-lined streets, including those near Prospect Park South and the historic Flatbush Reformed Church area, attract bees during warmer months. The neighborhood's mix of single-family Victorian homes, pre-war apartments, and commercial corridors along Flatbush Avenue and Church Avenue require specialized bee removal approaches tailored to each structure type.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a bee removal call in Flatbush?
faqs.answer: We typically respond to Flatbush bee removal requests within 24 hours, with same-day emergency service available for severe infestations or when bees pose immediate danger to residents, especially important in densely populated areas near schools and playgrounds.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 bee removal price include?
faqs.answer: Our pricing covers complete inspection, safe bee colony removal, hive extraction, entry point sealing, and cleanup. The final cost depends on hive size, location accessibility (attic, wall void, chimney), and whether structural repairs are needed after removal.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we humanely relocate bee colonies to designated apiaries where they can thrive safely away from residential areas. We only recommend extermination in cases where relocation isn't feasible due to aggressive Africanized bee behavior or severe structural risks.

faqs.question: Can bees return after removal in Flatbush homes?
faqs.answer: We prevent bee returns by completely removing all hive material and honey residue, then sealing entry points. Old hive pheromones can attract new swarms, so thorough cleanup and exclusion work is essential, particularly in older Flatbush homes with multiple potential entry points.
